title: leonohebe ramosissima
intro: Leonohebe ramosissima, also known as the hairy-leaved leonohebe, is a flowering plant species in the family Gesneriaceae. Native to New Zealand, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Leonohebe ramosissima include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional Maori medicine. Leonohebe ramosissima can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
